revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōmoto Kofun]][[ja:大元古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28685639]] Wikidata name: Ōmoto Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōmoto Kofun Cluster | native_name = 大元古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Ōmoto Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|42|5.77|N|131|52|4.16|E|region:JP-32_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.70160278|N|131.86782222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Masuda, Shimane|Masuda, Shimane]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in|San'in]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|5.77|N|131|52|4.16|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōmoto Kofun Cluster'''|大元古墳群|Ōmoto Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located on the Toda-chō neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Masuda, Shimane|Masuda]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2020.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/411755|title=大元古墳|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmoto Kofun cluster is located on a hilly ridge to the east of Masuda city in the western part of Shimane Prefecture. A survey was conducted from 1986 to 1988 and an [[archaeological excavation]] from 2015, confirming that the cluster consists of three ancient tumuli. The Ōmoto No.1 Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. With a length of 88 meters, it is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami region]]. It is orientated to the southwest and there is a ceremonial platform protruding from the north side of the mound. The posterior circular portion is 50 meters in diameter and seven meters in height and was constructed in four tiers. The anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters wide at the end and 24 meters at the construction, and 4.3 meters high and was constructed in two tiers. Rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were found on the surface, but no trace of moat was found. Based on the mound shape and excavated ''haniwa'', it is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century, or slightly earlier than the [[Sukumozuka Kofun]]. The Ōmoto No.2 Kofun is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} style Kofun, and is located adjacent to the front part of the Ōmoto No.1 Kofun. It has a diameter of 15 meters and is 1.2 meters high. Ōmoto No.3 Kofun is also a round ''enpun''-style mound. It has a diameter of 12 meters and is located to the west of the Ōmoto No.2 Kofun.
The tumuli were designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Sites in 1999, their status was elevated to a National Historic Sites in 2020. The site is located approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Masuda Station|Masuda Station]] [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://masudashi.com/kankouspot/kankouspot-1022/ Shimane Prefecture Masuda City Tourism Official Website]{{in lang|ja}}

The site is located approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Masuda Station|Masuda Station]] [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōmoto Kofun]][[ja:大元古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28685639]] Wikidata name: Ōmoto Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōmoto Kofun Cluster | native_name = 大元古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Ōmoto Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|42|5.77|N|131|52|4.16|E|region:JP-32_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.70160278|N|131.86782222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Masuda, Shimane|Masuda, Shimane]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in|San'in]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|5.77|N|131|52|4.16|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōmoto Kofun Cluster'''|大元古墳群|Ōmoto Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located on the Toda-chō neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Masuda, Shimane|Masuda]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2020.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/411755|title=大元古墳|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmoto Kofun cluster is located on a hilly ridge to the east of Masuda city in the western part of Shimane Prefecture. A survey was conducted from 1986 to 1988 and an [[archaeological excavation]] from 2015, confirming that the cluster consists of three ancient tumuli. The Ōmoto No.1 Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. With a length of 88 meters, it is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami region]]. It is orientated to the southwest and there is a ceremonial platform protruding from the north side of the mound. The posterior circular portion is 50 meters in diameter and seven meters in height and was constructed in four tiers. The anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters wide at the end and 24 meters at the construction, and 4.3 meters high and was constructed in two tiers. Rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were found on the surface, but no trace of moat was found. Based on the mound shape and excavated ''haniwa'', it is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century, or slightly earlier than the [[Sukumozuka Kofun]]. The Ōmoto No.2 Kofun is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} style Kofun, and is located adjacent to the front part of the Ōmoto No.1 Kofun. It has a diameter of 15 meters and is 1.2 meters high. Ōmoto No.3 Kofun is also a round ''enpun''-style mound. It has a diameter of 12 meters and is located to the west of the Ōmoto No.2 Kofun.
The tumuli were designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Sites in 1999, their status was elevated to a National Historic Sites in 2020. The site is located approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Masuda Station|Masuda Station]] [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

Based on the mound shape and excavated ''haniwa'', it is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century, or slightly earlier than the [[Sukumozuka Kofun]]. The Ōmoto No.2 Kofun is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} style Kofun, and is located adjacent to the front part of the Ōmoto No.1 Kofun. It has a diameter of 15 meters and is 1.2 meters high. Ōmoto No.3 Kofun is also a round ''enpun''-style mound. It has a diameter of 12 meters and is located to the west of the Ōmoto No.2 Kofun.
The tumuli were designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Sites in 1999, their status was elevated to a National Historic Sites in 2020. The site is located approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Masuda Station|Masuda Station]] [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].

The posterior circular portion is 50 meters in diameter and seven meters in height and was constructed in four tiers. Based on the mound shape and excavated ''haniwa'', it is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century, or slightly earlier than the [[Sukumozuka Kofun]]. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2020.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/411755|title=大元古墳|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmoto Kofun cluster is located on a hilly ridge to the east of Masuda city in the western part of Shimane Prefecture. A survey was conducted from 1986 to 1988 and an [[archaeological excavation]] from 2015, confirming that the cluster consists of three ancient tumuli. The Ōmoto No.1 Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. With a length of 88 meters, it is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami region]]. It is orientated to the southwest and there is a ceremonial platform protruding from the north side of the mound. The posterior circular portion is 50 meters in diameter and seven meters in height and was constructed in four tiers. The anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters wide at the end and 24 meters at the construction, and 4.3 meters high and was constructed in two tiers. Rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were found on the surface, but no trace of moat was found. Based on the mound shape and excavated ''haniwa'', it is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century, or slightly earlier than the [[Sukumozuka Kofun]]. The Ōmoto No.2 Kofun is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} style Kofun, and is located adjacent to the front part of the Ōmoto No.1 Kofun. It has a diameter of 15 meters and is 1.2 meters high. Ōmoto No.3 Kofun is also a round ''enpun''-style mound. It has a diameter of 12 meters and is located to the west of the Ōmoto No.2 Kofun.
The tumuli were designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Sites in 1999, their status was elevated to a National Historic Sites in 2020. The site is located approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Masuda Station|Masuda Station]] [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

The anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters wide at the end and 24 meters at the construction, and 4.3 meters high and was constructed in two tiers. Rows of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were found on the surface, but no trace of moat was found. Based on the mound shape and excavated ''haniwa'', it is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century, or slightly earlier than the [[Sukumozuka Kofun]]. The Ōmoto No.2 Kofun is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} style Kofun, and is located adjacent to the front part of the Ōmoto No.1 Kofun. It has a diameter of 15 meters and is 1.2 meters high. Ōmoto No.3 Kofun is also a round ''enpun''-style mound. It has a diameter of 12 meters and is located to the west of the Ōmoto No.2 Kofun.
The tumuli were designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Sites in 1999, their status was elevated to a National Historic Sites in 2020. The site is located approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Masuda Station|Masuda Station]] [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōmoto Kofun]][[ja:大元古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōmoto Kofun]][[ja:大元古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōmoto Kofun]][[ja:大元古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28685639]] Wikidata name: Ōmoto Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōmoto Kofun Cluster | native_name = 大元古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Ōmoto Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|42|5.77|N|131|52|4.16|E|region:JP-32_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.70160278|N|131.86782222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Masuda, Shimane|en|Masuda, Shimane|WD=Q753731|lt=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[:en:San'in|San'in]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|5.77|N|131|52|4.16|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōmoto Kofun Cluster'''|大元古墳群|Ōmoto Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located on the Toda-chō neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Masuda, Shimane|en|Masuda, Shimane|WD=Q753731|lt=Masuda}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2020.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/411755|title=大元古墳|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmoto Kofun cluster is located on a hilly ridge to the east of Masuda city in the western part of Shimane Prefecture. A survey was conducted from 1986 to 1988 and an [[archaeological excavation]] from 2015, confirming that the cluster consists of three ancient tumuli. The Ōmoto No.1 Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. With a length of 88 meters, it is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the {{ill|Iwami Province|en|Iwami Province|WD=Q1053709|lt=Iwami region}}. It is orientated to the southwest and there is a ceremonial platform protruding from the north side of the mound. The posterior circular portion is 50 meters in diameter and seven meters in height and was constructed in four tiers. The anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters wide at the end and 24 meters at the construction, and 4.3 meters high and was constructed in two tiers. Rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were found on the surface, but no trace of moat was found. Based on the mound shape and excavated ''haniwa'', it is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century, or slightly earlier than the [[Sukumozuka Kofun]]. The Ōmoto No.2 Kofun is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} style Kofun, and is located adjacent to the front part of the Ōmoto No.1 Kofun. It has a diameter of 15 meters and is 1.2 meters high. Ōmoto No.3 Kofun is also a round ''enpun''-style mound. It has a diameter of 12 meters and is located to the west of the Ōmoto No.2 Kofun.
The tumuli were designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Sites in 1999, their status was elevated to a National Historic Sites in 2020. The site is located approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Masuda Station|en|Masuda Station|WD=Q6785654|lt=Masuda Station}} {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://masudashi.com/kankouspot/kankouspot-1022/ Shimane Prefecture Masuda City Tourism Official Website]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōmoto Kofun Cluster | native_name = 大元古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Ōmoto Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|42|5.77|N|131|52|4.16|E|region:JP-32_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.70160278|N|131.86782222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Masuda, Shimane|en|Masuda, Shimane|WD=Q753731|lt=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[:en:San'in|San'in]] | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|5.77|N|131|52|4.16|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Ōmoto Kofun Cluster'''|大元古墳群|Ōmoto Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located on the Toda-chō neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Masuda, Shimane|en|Masuda, Shimane|WD=Q753731|lt=Masuda}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2020.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/411755|title=大元古墳|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmoto Kofun cluster is located on a hilly ridge to the east of Masuda city in the western part of Shimane Prefecture. A survey was conducted from 1986 to 1988 and an [[archaeological excavation]] from 2015, confirming that the cluster consists of three ancient tumuli. The Ōmoto No.1 Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. With a length of 88 meters, it is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the {{ill|Iwami Province|en|Iwami Province|WD=Q1053709|lt=Iwami region}}. It is orientated to the southwest and there is a ceremonial platform protruding from the north side of the mound. The posterior circular portion is 50 meters in diameter and seven meters in height and was constructed in four tiers. The anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters wide at the end and 24 meters at the construction, and 4.3 meters high and was constructed in two tiers. Rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were found on the surface, but no trace of moat was found. Based on the mound shape and excavated ''haniwa'', it is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century, or slightly earlier than the [[Sukumozuka Kofun]]. The Ōmoto No.2 Kofun is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} style Kofun, and is located adjacent to the front part of the Ōmoto No.1 Kofun. It has a diameter of 15 meters and is 1.2 meters high. Ōmoto No.3 Kofun is also a round ''enpun''-style mound. It has a diameter of 12 meters and is located to the west of the Ōmoto No.2 Kofun.
The tumuli were designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Sites in 1999, their status was elevated to a National Historic Sites in 2020. The site is located approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Masuda Station|en|Masuda Station|WD=Q6785654|lt=Masuda Station}} {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://masudashi.com/kankouspot/kankouspot-1022/ Shimane Prefecture Masuda City Tourism Official Website]{{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Ōmoto Kofun Cluster'''|大元古墳群|Ōmoto Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located on the Toda-chō neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Masuda, Shimane|en|Masuda, Shimane|WD=Q753731|lt=Masuda|1=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2020.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/411755|title=大元古墳|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmoto Kofun cluster is located on a hilly ridge to the east of Masuda city in the western part of Shimane Prefecture. A survey was conducted from 1986 to 1988 and an [[archaeological excavation]] from 2015, confirming that the cluster consists of three ancient tumuli. The Ōmoto No.1 Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. With a length of 88 meters, it is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the {{ill|Iwami Province|en|Iwami Province|WD=Q1053709|lt=Iwami region|1=Iwami Province}}. It is orientated to the southwest and there is a ceremonial platform protruding from the north side of the mound. The posterior circular portion is 50 meters in diameter and seven meters in height and was constructed in four tiers. The anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters wide at the end and 24 meters at the construction, and 4.3 meters high and was constructed in two tiers. Rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were found on the surface, but no trace of moat was found. Based on the mound shape and excavated ''haniwa'', it is estimated to have been built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century, or slightly earlier than the [[Sukumozuka Kofun]]. The Ōmoto No.2 Kofun is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} style Kofun, and is located adjacent to the front part of the Ōmoto No.1 Kofun. It has a diameter of 15 meters and is 1.2 meters high. Ōmoto No.3 Kofun is also a round ''enpun''-style mound. It has a diameter of 12 meters and is located to the west of the Ōmoto No.2 Kofun.
The tumuli were designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Sites in 1999, their status was elevated to a National Historic Sites in 2020. The site is located approximately ten minutes by car from {{ill|Masuda Station|en|Masuda Station|WD=Q6785654|lt=Masuda Station|1=Masuda Station}} {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://masudashi.com/kankouspot/kankouspot-1022/ Shimane Prefecture Masuda City Tourism Official Website]{{in lang|ja}}
